title: buildah build fails on Kind Cluster with message: fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
body:
**Description**

quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.23.1 running as a container within Kind fails when doing a build with the following message:

"fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.  Run buildah build --tag $IMAGE_NAME .
2.
3.


**Describe the results you received:**
The build process fails after Writing manifest to image destination with a message:  error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/... (file paths omitted for brevity)

Full Message:
```
Storing signatures
time="2022-01-06T02:01:09Z" level=error msg="error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/b38c1f7cb5be5f1c186292c76ecee594851207b475a2d02a87a278337635fd5d/merged: invalid argument"
error mounting new container: error mounting build container "4508d36e2430534372a7a4fe8d8d5f80cbfb1dfa5da39c57d472a14f4b934dff": error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/b38c1f7cb5be5f1c186292c76ecee594851207b475a2d02a87a278337635fd5d/merged, mount_data="nodev,fsync=0,lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/UVNMRZHJA66WPE4JRY775ZWPEQ:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/QNHAU65GZKJVU7XTDKZVDBKZFH:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/2WSJZXYF4B7JVUTUDNUQERKGB5:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/7NVDPBXGN5RH5XXLYBI4XXNPCC:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/V5XERDZF7R4Z4IHVDB5PFN6NFO:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/JEO6RP7ZLMZ4U3QRQQMO5HJOPB:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/LQEPB4GIAWCM2XKFJN344VJ362:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/XIDCFLIXQD6NWA4WMEMPYMAPKT:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/H6RFVY2MKXWEJE55ZK3ZOKCVQY:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/FRRGCOA4DPLE4YGGZSRAZD2E76:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/UTUUSCMGPKZRZ2O7O3YE5EGITO:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/SPJPNYZJTVQWSRFPATFCLELMJT:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/FA3N4OB3IM54ZPWRXXD46IR53V:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/5MOUFYX7UGU6MXCDNEYW53ETF2:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/56A5OWVK3NBK5CSQI7JZOTY2ZR:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/EDGSC2NWS44OIMFQIZLUZF6J5L:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/7YXJIXRQ2FLZNSVXO4I4C3CR3Y:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/RHAAFRDQJLT5Q4GRH7WG7UPTMP:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/AUWXEIDX63NSB3EJSWTLOVTYZT:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/6BI6EFOZPB6ZUWU3UNFVGRYMO6:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/7FBI4WOXNJD2P6UAJHLXODKXQJ:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/E3VCFQKREMNC4NN6B7TDPDO7R7:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/32BL2MF6EBZED7WZFYA6GQHD63:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GARBDCT2VFILRXHUZQ7QT32YAB:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/G3LKW5QUT3H4BZYFCBIXWPFWDS:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/FMPDWA7WFKEAB5KOOHM4RTOWAU:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/I2LBA6I5EQ3JZIMQOJEEXL7J7Z:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/5MG2LMARTMLP7JFJ5HK5H6NTT3:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/DDQLDWPLG3PND42G4C5QEEYIWC:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/U7F3C6Y5SZL4SAEYDR4PSDDRBT:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/MJHMEVBXT4W3ALKVVMOYBMZXTB:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/4IYSDTAO6UNNFGRHCELDKYIWJ5:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/MRO2OBRMM74GNPLJYH2QYH74AW:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/KHCXAJA3MCNNUIQ2ZCLVNI3TSG:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZLIPDOGOVVZZ4FL6BCP5XC25UW:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/XWTR3OZMF6GDPDE2PJBTV6MBJB:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/TKQPGY4CZJKWWK25N5QYRERYEY:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/B5AGVIMGLDSDK46JVFH2UKKBFE:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/SOO5P7AKI43IW3FYWXTLLQNC4T:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/SMB6PTIDSMA47N7AOPJOY4KR5I:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/7V7LQUOBC66UB2SEK5UWW23BNC:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/JLF4URDANQHFG5TFQCXG7IZBKJ:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/5Y6O7PCF5KIKY4HDI7VPRBW62X:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/PEWCLR5ICCNTQFKVFRN4DNNCM5:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/PBJS57J6FMTXUC2O6QBJOMUJ2W:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/BQ2ZWAWTZUXMH3QDEVFSHABMKH:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/4F3SVVXZPXL24I44XEKZE2TS5L:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/WKDJ36S2VT4NLR37XLERNDZQ46:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/XF5PD67IGZ3R2SCWQ5M3PRRXKH:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/4P5AG7RLBRHZ5FSI4GGYQROFFX:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/QJ6LQ6BDMWYJZ4P6JGUHUU662H:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/WGH6HJGXNIKVTGR4UKJJRKQ4OL:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/NYHOTCGU4ATLCQ4IOTH6AL2AR2:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/WKAKUR6RPQ4PK4BJNAFGVIGJXM:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/BRILNQUGDF2VAD2M3K4VKPWUXR:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/FNLPFCAMPCMBKDYKWU6BLIG6U6:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/TKVFCGLDVUHVTUEWW55BBZQRG4:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/DGW5YFJV3EIPLELCUIAAOC2WEF:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/TLEUS6SC3RKZQGWUFRIQX5QMOO:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/A4653MFNBWSWLEWMOEKNDQPRR2:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/FQFTKXAC7MMY3JZUJKY3Q632QO:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZEBXTRN5OQB46QNVI3DZZI3OL2:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/BMECS5FQQH3A2S7TQX5RYTQPVN:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/SKFB4TDTQLTI7OGDVYDWLY672R:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/PRWHFC4ECENOEPMSR3LV2JYB7L:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZIH6ETLEYDOKLEILNEVZS5VP47:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/J56EXI43SQV22NDZA6KWHVAOEQ:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/YGHPGJWNTVIOISKZ3NTXGVCF6W:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZFGNEE7NEPWH3RQXTIPE2R2NPQ:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/JLIRSLIMK5BUXPIO4YLXLYP7WC:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/74WU7D3CVXFBWQ4WCPEKM3DP6C:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/AZRU5DVKGKH3DM5UDZTIKKJ6HB:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/J3Q7DL5SJEYWVKK3DCRKLWRKKY:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/BEN6P6TUOENRF2VLH5U7RJCZ5W:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GN4E2ANVSDTSFULCHVMZTHIHAY:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/QSTX4OABVAE7ZDTSV5CD44IHG2:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/QWKPCYALJU23ZJRAEKWZTA7YIE:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/YGE6GKKXOSCGVGVZAYXNDED6DK:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/P7XYWAX67ZCVLDXOG57JUKOFBL:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/QTP6YTGUDVQBW4MVWZH3VBL4PW:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/WNPPU6ZL5PXXCBGENSGSFZRDML:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/L7W42NGG4V6KYULRNPTQKTVFLE:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/USF4NDCF3VSSIRT5DFTS6DNIBI:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/23NXGCXXRQFCVAFCFSS3OKFVZF:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/NSJYZYZ7UZGWQU2RPGVYR7QECH:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GCQWHSUBZJJ53CICBJ7Z7RS7J6,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/b38c1f7cb5be5f1c186292c76ecee594851207b475a2d02a87a278337635fd5d/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/b38c1f7cb5be5f1c186292c76ecee594851207b475a2d02a87a278337635fd5d/work,volatile": using mount program /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs: fuse: device not found, try 'modprobe fuse' first
fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
: exit status 1
time="2022-01-06T02:01:09Z" level=error msg="exit status 125"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected Podman to finish correctly

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.23.1-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.23.1
Go Version:      go1.16.8
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev

image Version:   5.16.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Tue Sep 28 18:26:37 2021
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 35 (Thirty Five)
```

```
Linux 7692467eae1e 5.10.76-linuxkit #1 SMP Mon Nov 8 10:21:19 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# This file is is the configuration file for all tools
# that use the containers/storage library.
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information
# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver, Must be set for proper operation.
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

# Storage path for rootless users
#
# rootless_storage_path = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# Storage options to be passed to underlying storage drivers

# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
"/var/lib/shared",
]

# Remap-UIDs/GIDs is the mapping from UIDs/GIDs as they should appear inside of
# a container, to the UIDs/GIDs as they should appear outside of the container,
# and the length of the range of UIDs/GIDs.  Additional mapped sets can be
# listed and will be heeded by libraries, but there are limits to the number of
# mappings which the kernel will allow when you later attempt to run a
# container.
#
# remap-uids = 0:1668442479:65536
# remap-gids = 0:1668442479:65536

# Remap-User/Group is a user name which can be used to look up one or more UID/GID
# ranges in the /etc/subuid or /etc/subgid file.  Mappings are set up starting
# with an in-container ID of 0 and then a host-level ID taken from the lowest
# range that matches the specified name, and using the length of that range.
# Additional ranges are then assigned, using the ranges which specify the
# lowest host-level IDs first, to the lowest not-yet-mapped in-container ID,
# until all of the entries have been used for maps.
#
# remap-user = "containers"
# remap-group = "containers"

# Root-auto-userns-user is a user name which can be used to look up one or more UID/GID
# ranges in the /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid file.  These ranges will be partitioned
# to containers configured to create automatically a user namespace.  Containers
# configured to automatically create a user namespace can still overlap with containers
# having an explicit mapping set.
# This setting is ignored when running as rootless.
# root-auto-userns-user = "storage"
#
# Auto-userns-min-size is the minimum size for a user namespace created automatically.
# auto-userns-min-size=1024
#
# Auto-userns-max-size is the minimum size for a user namespace created automatically.
# auto-userns-max-size=65536

[storage.options.overlay]
# ignore_chown_errors can be set to allow a non privileged user running with
# a single UID within a user namespace to run containers. The user can pull
# and use any image even those with multiple uids.  Note multiple UIDs will be
# squashed down to the default uid in the container.  These images will have no
# separation between the users in the container. Only supported for the overlay
# and vfs drivers.
#ignore_chown_errors = "false"

# Inodes is used to set a maximum inodes of the container image.
# inodes = ""

# Path to an helper program to use for mounting the file system instead of mounting it
# directly.
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"

# mountopt specifies comma separated list of extra mount options
mountopt = "nodev,fsync=0"

# Set to skip a PRIVATE bind mount on the storage home directory.
# skip_mount_home = "false"

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.
# size = ""

# ForceMask specifies the permissions mask that is used for new files and
# directories.
#
# The values "shared" and "private" are accepted.
# Octal permission masks are also accepted.
#
#  "": No value specified.
#     All files/directories, get set with the permissions identified within the
#     image.
#  "private": it is equivalent to 0700.
#     All files/directories get set with 0700 permissions.  The owner has rwx
#     access to the files. No other users on the system can access the files.
#     This setting could be used with networked based homedirs.
#  "shared": it is equivalent to 0755.
#     The owner has rwx access to the files and everyone else can read, access
#     and execute them. This setting is useful for sharing containers storage
#     with other users.  For instance have a storage owned by root but shared
#     to rootless users as an additional store.
#     NOTE:  All files within the image are made readable and executable by any
#     user on the system. Even /etc/shadow within your image is now readable by
#     any user.
#
#   OCTAL: Users can experiment with other OCTAL Permissions.
#
#  Note: The force_mask Flag is an experimental feature, it could change in the
#  future.  When "force_mask" is set the original permission mask is stored in
#  the "user.containers.override_stat" xattr and the "mount_program" option must
#  be specified. Mount programs like "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs" present the
#  extended attribute permissions to processes within containers rather then the
#  "force_mask"  permissions.
#
# force_mask = ""

[storage.options.thinpool]
# Storage Options for thinpool

# autoextend_percent determines the amount by which pool needs to be
# grown. This is specified in terms of % of pool size. So a value of 20 means
# that when threshold is hit, pool will be grown by 20% of existing
# pool size.
# autoextend_percent = "20"

# autoextend_threshold determines the pool extension threshold in terms
# of percentage of pool size. For example, if threshold is 60, that means when
# pool is 60% full, threshold has been hit.
# autoextend_threshold = "80"

# basesize specifies the size to use when creating the base device, which
# limits the size of images and containers.
# basesize = "10G"

# blocksize specifies a custom blocksize to use for the thin pool.
# blocksize="64k"

# directlvm_device specifies a custom block storage device to use for the
# thin pool. Required if you setup devicemapper.
# directlvm_device = ""

# directlvm_device_force wipes device even if device already has a filesystem.
# directlvm_device_force = "True"

# fs specifies the filesystem type to use for the base device.
# fs="xfs"

# log_level sets the log level of devicemapper.
# 0: LogLevelSuppress 0 (Default)
# 2: LogLevelFatal
# 3: LogLevelErr
# 4: LogLevelWarn
# 5: LogLevelNotice
# 6: LogLevelInfo
# 7: LogLevelDebug
# log_level = "7"

# min_free_space specifies the min free space percent in a thin pool require for
# new device creation to succeed. Valid values are from 0% - 99%.
# Value 0% disables
# min_free_space = "10%"

# mkfsarg specifies extra mkfs arguments to be used when creating the base
# device.
# mkfsarg = ""

# metadata_size is used to set the `pvcreate --metadatasize` options when
# creating thin devices. Default is 128k
# metadata_size = ""

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.
# size = ""

# use_deferred_removal marks devicemapper block device for deferred removal.
# If the thinpool is in use when the driver attempts to remove it, the driver
# tells the kernel to remove it as soon as possible. Note this does not free
# up the disk space, use deferred deletion to fully remove the thinpool.
# use_deferred_removal = "True"

# use_deferred_deletion marks thinpool device for deferred deletion.
# If the device is busy when the driver attempts to delete it, the driver
# will attempt to delete device every 30 seconds until successful.
# If the program using the driver exits, the driver will continue attempting
# to cleanup the next time the driver is used. Deferred deletion permanently
# deletes the device and all data stored in device will be lost.
# use_deferred_deletion = "True"

# xfs_nospace_max_retries specifies the maximum number of retries XFS should
# attempt to complete IO when ENOSPC (no space) error is returned by
# underlying storage device.
# xfs_nospace_max_retries = "0"
```

comments.body:
@joshuawhite929 Hi thanks for creating issue.

Could you share more info , possibly the `yaml` you applied

It looks like your setup is missing `fuse-overlay` daemon-set. Please apply it: https://github.com/kuberenetes-learning-group/fuse-device-plugin/blob/master/fuse-device-plugin-k8s-1.16.yml

If you don't want to apply daemon-set give your pod privileges and capabilities and run as `root` .

This works for me without `fuse-overlay`
```yaml
    ...
    securityContext:
       capabilities:
         add:
           - "SYS_ADMIN"
           - "MKNOD"
           - "SYS_CHROOT"
           - "SETFCAP"
```

@flouthoc My usage is very basic.  I simply took the quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.23.1 image, extended it to add bash and skopeo and haven't made any other configuration changes.

I got this image to run on Docker with the addition of these arguments: 
`docker run --security-opt seccomp:unconfined --device /dev/fuse`

I wasn't sure if something similar was necessary for running it within a Kind cluster or eventually EKS.  If it wasn't clear, I am new to Buildah.  I'm having difficulty trying to find out where some of these intricacies are documented.

Hey Josh.  You would need to do the same.

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes

Might give you some ideas. While these are Podman, Buildah is similar.

Buildah does have a nice feature to make this more secure with 

buildah build --isolation=chroot ...
With more recent kernels, you should not need fuse-overlayfs either.

I'd like to make this work because I am already using skopeo and would love to be able to share the secrets/certificates.  With that said, I come to buildah from kaniko where I can simply run a container and build images without the need for additional configuration.  Is there a way to get to that model with buildah?  For example, I'd love to be able to wire up a [Buildah Tekton task](https://hub.tekton.dev/tekton/task/buildah) and be done.  Am I doing it wrong or is this not possible without additional configuration?
@joshuawhite929 I know enough about Kind to be deemed completely and totally ignorant.  Is it possible to do what you're trying to do with Podman rather than Buildah inside of Kind?

That's an option.  Because all I am doing is extending a container, I initially thought having an entire runtime would be overkill.  If it's an easier option to get working though, I'll give it a try.  If I remember right, podman also has an "inspect" command which would let me swap out skopeo.

@joshuawhite929 yep, Skopeo has an inspect command and does a lot of what Skopeo does.  You indeed might be able to merge the work that you're doing with both Skopeo and Buildah to just using Podman.  FWIW, Buildah is used by Podman under the covers to build the images.

No Buildah and Podman within a container have the same level of complexity.

buildah build --isolation chroot ...

Should be the easiest path.

@joshuawhite929 Did you look at those two articles I sent?

@rhatdan - One more thing... I am experiencing this headache when using [Kind](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/).  Looking at the [Dockerfile that they use](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind/blob/main/images/base/Dockerfile), don't they already use the fuse-overlayfs?

Buildah needs some privs in order to mount, which means it needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN or CAP_SETUID/CAP_SETGID in order to create a user namespace and mount namespace.  It also usually needs > 1 UID within its User Namespace, so it can assign root and all UIDs required to the the build.  I know of no way that Kaniko could do this without requiring the same.

@nalind @giuseppe Thoughts?

In order to use fuse you also need to leak /dev/fuse into the container.

@flouthoc @umohnani8 have you tried this?

Yes, I have mounted /dev/fuse into the container when running podman inside a container in a kubernetes cluster. The steps are under the `rootless podman without the privileged flag` section in the article here https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes.
The article might help with some of the configurations needed to podman inside a container with a kind set up as well.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
